rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Stephen Sturla And Daniel Marc Bornstein And Exclusion Of Expert Testimony. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE JUDGE PRO TEM RECUSES HIMSELF FROM THIS CASE. The motion is continued to September 9, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to MSchreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GREG CONTOS, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. KOKKARI LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT KOKKARI LTD, GEORGE MARCUS, KENNETH FRANGANDAKIS' Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's Motion for Partial Judgment on the Pleadings is DENIED. Defendants' Motion for Judgment on the pleadings is improper because Plaintiff's Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against Defendants. (Cal. Code Civ. Pro. § 438(c)(1)(B).) Defendants allege that Plaintiff's remaining Fourth Cause of Action is barred because he did not exhaust his Private Attorney General Act ("PAGA") claims. (Labor Code § 2698 et seq.) with the Labor and Workforce Development Agency ("LWDA"). Plaintiff, however, has satisfied requirements for administrative exhaustion under Labor Code Section 2699.3 by providing to Defendant and to the LWDA written notice of his intent to seek penalties for Defendants' alleged Labor Code violations. Plaintiff in his notice alleged that defendants violated Labor Code Section 510 for failing to pay overtime wages and Section 512 for failing to provide meal breaks. Discovery requests further elucidated Plaintiff's theories as to these causes of actions. Plaintiff's written notice provided to LWDA and defendants sufficient information about the facts and theories of his claim so as to allow Defendant to devise an appropriate defense and to allow LWDA to make an informed decision as to whether to investigate Plaintiff's claim. Defendants further allege that this Court must dismiss plaintiff's claim for penalties under section Labor Code section 226 because Plaintiff cannot recover both under the PAGA as well as under section 226. The PAG Act, however provides that an aggrieved employee may collect penalties for those Labor Code sections that have a penalty provision and for those Labor Code sections enumerated in the PAGA. (Labor Code § 2699(g): "Nothing in this part shall operate to limit an employees' right to pursue or recover other remedies available under state or federal law, either separately or concurrently with an action taken under this part.") Defendants seek a judgment on the pleadings as to Plaintiff's Fourth Cause of Action, to which this Court already overruled Defendants' demurrer. Defendants, furthermore, do not address plaintiff's claim that defendants are liable for penalties under Labor Code section 558, which is not enumerated in the PAGA. Not all statutory penalties are "civil penalties" subject to the exhaustion requirements of the PAGA. (Dunlap v. Superior Court (2006) 142 Cal. App. 4th 330, 339.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Nicholas Prepouses And Mary Prepouses' Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 4, Defendants Nicholas Prepouses And Mary Prepouses' Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO, GRANTED; RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, DUE FOURTEEN DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ORDER. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED AS THE MOTION WAS NOT UNSUCCESSFULLY OPPOSED. SEE CCP Secs. 2030.290(c), 2031.300(c) AND Trans-Action Commercial Investors, Ltd. v Firmaterr Inc. (1997) 60 CAL.APP.4th 352, 355.) rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MURRAY JR. VS. ANOUSHIRAVAN MASSOUMI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Costs Bill
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 5, Motion To Strike Costs Bill. The motion to strike the costs memorandum is granted. Judge McBride clearly ruled that "[n]either side shall be deemed a prevailing party." (Statement of Decision, 7:19). Plaintiff Murray fails to demonstrate that CCP 998 is a basis to recover his costs. Nothing in the statement of decision clearly states the value of the property at the time of judgment and the amount of equity is uncertain. It is unclear whether the judgment or acceptance of plaintiff Murray's CCP 998 offer would lead to a better result for defendant Massoumi. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE HARRIS VS. DISCOUNT BEAUTY SUPPLY AKA BEAUTY SUPPLY WARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 8, PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE HARRIS' Motion To Reconsider. The motion is denied. Plaintiff failed to comply with the requirements of CCP 1008. Plaintiff does not present any new facts, law, or circumstances to warrant reconsideration. Plaintiff did not oppose the motion for terminating sanctions or contest the tentative ruling. Plaintiff was properly deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem hearing the matter because she did not raise a timely objection. rulings.Case Title: JANET FIORE VS. KEVIN WALLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Of Defendants Kevin Wallace And Wallace Remodeling, Inc.; Memo Points And Auth.; Decl.; Prop. Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANTS KEVIN WALLACE AND WALLACE REMODELING INC.'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING, THE PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER REGARDING AND BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS AT THE HEARING THE PRIVACY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES, SUCH AS EMPLOYEES OF THE DEFENDANTS POTENTIALLY IMPLICATED BY THE THIRD PARTY SUBPOENAS IN THIS MATTER, WHETHER MORE NARROWLY DRAWN REQUESTS MAY PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH THE INFORMATION SHE NEEDS WITHOUT THE POTENTIAL FOR INVADING THE PRIVACY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES, AND HOW THE COURT SHOULD BALANCE THE COMPETING INTERESTS OF THE RIGHTS OF LITIGANTS TO DISCOVERY AND THE RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES TO PRIVACY UNDER THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION. (See Valley Bank of Nevada v. Superior Court (1975) 15 Cal.3d 652, AND Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 40 Cal.4th 360. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to MSchreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITIONS GRANTED; SANCTIONS AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,010. DEFENDANTS DID NOT APPEAR AT A PROPERLY NOTICED DEPOSITION HAVING FILED NEITHER A MOTION TO STAY/QUASH THE DEPOSITION NOTICE NOR A MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER. rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN REG VS. NAPA STATE HOSPITAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of California Department Of State Hospitals Dolly Matteucci And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN REG'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of California Department Of State Hospitals Dolly Matteucci And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. PARTIES TO APPEAR WITH PROPOSED ORDERS AND SHOULD BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS THE APPLICABILITY OF Westly v. Superior Court (2004), 125 Cal.App.4th 907 AND Nagle v. Superior Court (1994), 28 Cal. App.4th 1465. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to MSchreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RELEVAD CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. QIMEDIA, INC., DBA QI MEDIA,A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANT RELEVAD CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Causes of action one and two sufficiently allege a breach of contract claim. "Although breach of the implied covenant often is pleaded as a separate count, a breach of the implied covenant is necessarily a breach of contract." Digerati Holdings, LLC v. Young Money Entertainment, LLC (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 873, 885. Cause of action three sufficiently alleges concealment based upon Relevad's active concealment and/or exclusive knowledge. See Limandri v. Judkins (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 326, 336-337. Cause of action four alleges the intentional misrepresentation cause of action with sufficient particularity. Q1Media alleges that Relevad misrepresented that the bot clicks were human clicks from 3/14 - 11/14. (Pars. 55-56 of the Cross-Complaint). Those allegations are sufficiently particular and less specificity is required where the cross-defendant "necessarily possesses full information concerning the facts of the controversy." Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216-217. Cause of action 8 alleges the violation of the UCL claim with reasonable particularity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION VS. ARCTOUCH INCORPORATED, A CORPORATION AKA ARCTOUCH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. In ruling on a demurrer, courts must take all material facts pleaded as true. (See Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 992, 998.) Here, the Cross-Complaint alleges: (1) Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a contract; (2) Plaintiff breached the contract by failing to perform; and (3) Defendant has been harmed in an amount in excess of $250,000. (See Cross-Complaint 37- 41.) Therefore the first COA has been properly pled and the demurrer is overruled. "There is an implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract that neither party will do anything which will injure the right of the other to receive the benefits of the agreement." (Comunale v. Traders & General Ins. Co. (1958) 50 Cal.2d 654, 658, internal citation omitted.) Here, Defendant has properly pled that Plaintiff breached the covenant of good faith and fair dealing by interfering with Defendant's right to receive benefits under the agreement. Therefore the demurrer to the second COA is overruled. Finally, Defendant has pled Plaintiff made false representations that Defendant relied upon and as a result Defendant was harmed. Thus, the demurrer to the third COA is overruled. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION VS. ARCTOUCH INCORPORATED, A CORPORATION AKA ARCTOUCH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 28, 2015, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Motion to Strike is granted in part. Under CCP §436, movant must show the pleading to contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. As to paragraphs 11, 13-14, 17, and 23 of the Cross-Complaint, the Court finds the Cross-Complaint does not contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. As to paragraphs 27-36 of the Cross-Complaint, the Court finds the Cross-Complaint does not contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. Plainitff/Cross-Defendant's motion to strike damages for $250,000 is denied. Plainitff/Cross-Defendant's motion to strike request for attorney's fees is granted with 20 days leave to amend.
